purpose.summary: The COPAUA0C program updates a pending authorization summary and inserts authorization details into an IMS database. It retrieves the current date and time from CICS, calculates authorization dates and times, and populates the pending authorization details segment with request and response data. The program also handles IMS database updates and error logging.
purpose.business_context: This program is likely part of a larger authorization processing system, handling the persistence of authorization requests and responses.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY
inputs.description: Contains summary information about pending authorizations for an account.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
inputs.description: Contains detailed information about a specific pending authorization.

inputs.name: PA-RL-AUTH-ID-CODE
inputs.description: Authorization ID code from the response.

outputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY
outputs.description: Updated summary information about pending authorizations.

outputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
outputs.description: Detailed information about the pending authorization inserted into the IMS database.

business_rules.description: If the authorization response is approved, increment the approved authorization count and amount in the pending authorization summary.
business_rules.logic_summary: Adds 1 to PA-APPROVED-AUTH-CNT and WS-APPROVED-AMT to PA-APPROVED-AUTH-AMT if AUTH-RESP-APPROVED is true.

business_rules.description: If the authorization response is declined, increment the declined authorization count and amount in the pending authorization summary.
business_rules.logic_summary: Adds 1 to PA-DECLINED-AUTH-CNT and PA-TRANSACTION-AMT to PA-DECLINED-AUTH-AMT if AUTH-RESP-APPROVED is false.

transforms.transformation_description: Multiplies WS-CUR-TIME-N6 by 1000 and adds WS-CUR-TIME-MS.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph is the main driver for writing authorization information to the database. It orchestrates the update of the pending authorization summary and the insertion of the authorization details. It first calls 8400-UPDATE-SUMMARY to update the summary information. Then, it calls 8500-INSERT-AUTH to insert the detailed authorization record. No direct data manipulation or error handling is performed within this paragraph; it serves as a control point for the two sub-processes. The paragraph consumes no direct input and produces no direct output other than triggering the two subordinate paragraphs.

This paragraph simply provides an exit point for the 8000-WRITE-AUTH-TO-DB paragraph, allowing for a structured return. It does not perform any operations or data manipulation. It serves as a standard COBOL exit paragraph.

This paragraph updates the pending authorization summary in the IMS database. It first checks if a summary segment already exists (NFOUND-PAUT-SMRY-SEG). If not, it initializes the PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY segment. It then moves account and customer IDs, credit limits, and cash credit limits into the summary segment. Based on whether the authorization was approved (AUTH-RESP-APPROVED), it increments the approved or declined authorization counts and amounts. Finally, it either updates (REPL) or inserts (ISRT) the PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY segment into the IMS database using EXEC DLI commands. Error handling is performed by checking the IMS return code (DIBSTAT) and calling 9500-LOG-ERROR if the update or insert fails.

This paragraph provides an exit point for the 8400-UPDATE-SUMMARY paragraph, enabling structured control flow. It does not perform any data manipulation or processing. It serves as a standard COBOL exit paragraph.

This paragraph inserts authorization details into the IMS database. It first retrieves the current date and time from CICS using EXEC CICS ASKTIME and FORMATTIME commands, converting them into usable formats. It then calculates authorization dates and times by subtracting the current date and time from a maximum value. The paragraph moves data from request and response fields (PA-RQ-* and PA-RL-*) into the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment. It sets flags based on whether the authorization was approved or declined. Finally, it inserts the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment into the IMS database using an EXEC DLI ISRT command. Error handling is performed by checking the IMS return code (DIBSTAT) and calling 9500-LOG-ERROR if the insert fails.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 8500-INSERT-AUTH paragraph, ensuring structured program flow. It performs no data processing or manipulation. It is a standard COBOL exit paragraph.

This paragraph handles the termination of the program. It first checks if the IMS PSB is scheduled (IMS-PSB-SCHD) and, if so, terminates the IMS connection using EXEC DLI TERM. It then calls 9100-CLOSE-REQUEST-QUEUE to close the request message queue. This paragraph ensures proper cleanup and resource release before the program ends.

This paragraph provides a standard exit point for the 9000-TERMINATE paragraph, maintaining structured program control. It does not perform any data manipulation or processing. It is a typical COBOL exit paragraph.

This paragraph closes the request message queue. It checks if the request message queue is open (WS-REQUEST-MQ-OPEN). If it is, it calls the MQCLOSE program to close the queue, passing the connection handle (W01-HCONN-REQUEST), object handle (W01-HOBJ-REQUEST), and other parameters. It then checks the completion code (WS-COMPCODE). If the close operation is successful (MQCC-OK), it sets WS-REQUEST-MQ-CLSE to TRUE. If the close operation fails, it logs an error using 9500-LOG-ERROR.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 9100-CLOSE-REQUEST-QUEUE paragraph, ensuring structured program flow. It does not perform any data processing or manipulation. It is a standard COBOL exit paragraph.

error_handling.action: Logs an error with location I003, sets error flags, and calls 9500-LOG-ERROR.
Logs an error with location I004, sets error flags, and calls 9500-LOG-ERROR.
Logs an error with location M005, sets error flags, and calls 9500-LOG-ERROR.

cics_operations.purpose: Retrieves the current date and time from CICS.
Formats the CICS time into usable date and time formats.

open_questions.question: What is the purpose of the 99999 - WS-YYDDD and 999999999 - WS-TIME-WITH-MS computations?
open_questions.context: The purpose of subtracting the date and time from a large number is unclear without more context. It might be related to date/time comparisons or encryption.
open_questions.suggestion: Review the data dictionary or related documentation to understand the purpose of these calculations.

open_questions.context: The code calls 9500-LOG-ERROR in several places, but the code for that paragraph is not provided.
